content: If we treat LLM self-reflection mechanisms like the learning-focused writing reflection exercises in high school English curricula, we can design a structured sequential self-reflection framework for LLMs that guides them to first map errors in their output, then revise their reasoning, just as students revise essays to align their arguments with their core reflective goals. This framework leverages the graded feedback structure of writing workshops to reduce unproductive random self-reflection in large language models.
